items.title: cuenv: one typed file for your whole project
items.summary: Most projects don't have a configuration system — they have a pile:  a .env file, a Makefile, a CI workflow, and secrets in a fourth place, none of it validated and all of it slowly drifting apart. cuenv replaces that pile with a single typed env.cue written in CUE. In this overview I'll show you how it validates your config before anything runs, resolves secrets at runtime, runs your tasks as a cached DAG, and generates your CI from the same definitions.
